title: rhoicissus dimidiata
intro: Rhoicissus dimidiata, also known as the half-flanged vine or half-flanged raphion, is a plant species native to Southern Africa. It is a woody climber with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various ailments. The plant's extracts have been found to possess anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating wounds. Rhoicissus dimidiata is often used in traditional herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties.
